title: Lane C uncovered typing inventory
summary: Keep the lane-C uncovered typing slice local to the claimed registry, devtools, and simulation files; make the owned production files pyright-clean without touching pyproject.toml; and capture the exact future strict-move candidates for lane D.
acceptance_criteria: Owned production files are pyright-clean without modifying pyproject.toml.
Focused owner tests for fault injection, property-based parser checks, and SFC simulation pass.
The handoff records the real changed files, validation state, and exact files that lane D should later move into tool.pyright.strict.
Registry work stays local to the new shim files so the concurrently claimed registry.py surface remains untouched.
risks: The new registry shim files are preparatory surfaces only; registry.py remains the controlling implementation until its active concurrent claim is resolved.
Lane D still needs to move the validated uncovered files into tool.pyright.strict because this slice intentionally leaves pyproject.toml unchanged.
validation: & ".venv/Scripts/pyright.exe" src/sattlint/analyzers/_registry_delivery.py src/sattlint/analyzers/_registry_specs.py src/sattlint/devtools/fault_injection.py src/sattlint/devtools/fuzzer.py src/sattlint/devtools/property_tests.py src/sattlint/simulation/__init__.py src/sattlint/simulation/_runtime_models.py src/sattlint/simulation/runtime.py
